title: Extend validateRelations to detect cycles in authored relations
body: POC v2 validateRelations() catches dangling-id cases (edge_unresolved_child, edge_parent_wrong_block, edge_parent_not_in_bins, edge_unknown_relation_type) but does NOT detect cycles in (parent, child, relation_type) triples. Cycles in hierarchy edges create infinite drill-down for any consumer walking the hierarchy. Cycles in lens edges are nonsensical (a bin is its own item?). Validator should walk each relation_type subgraph (Tarjan or simple DFS with visited+stack) and emit edge_cycle_detected diagnostics with the cycle path. Authoring-time validation prevents the issue rather than handling it at runtime via guards (which is the issue-072 concern). Surfaced as gap #28 in the integration-map review.
resolved_by: Resolved in commit 8059764 (substrate SDK port). validateRelations final-pass cycle detection landed with edge_cycle_detected diagnostic shape { code, message, cycle: string[], relation_type } per the issue spec. Six tests cover 2-node, 3-node, self-loop, multi-component, acyclic-control, partition-by-relation_type. Status was open in error; this update brings the lifecycle in sync with the implementation reality.
